rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Judgment Debtors Motion For Appointment Of Receiver
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 2, Judgment Debtor's Motion For Appointment Of Receiver. If oral argument is requested, the hearing will be at 9am before Judge Kahn. Defendant and judgment debtor Anne Kihagi's motion for appointment of Saul Armian as receiver is denied. As stated in the order granting the motion of plaintiffs and judgment creditors Dale Duncan and Marta Mendoza's for the appointment of Kevin Whelan as receiver, plaintiffs' choice of receiver is entitled to great weight and there has been no showing sufficient to overcome the presumption that Mr. Whelan will faithfully comply with his oath and all court orders. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE DUNCAN ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Hearing Re: Receiver Appointment And Fees. (Specially Set At 9am Before Judge Kahn)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 2, Hearing Re: Receiver Appointment And Fees. If oral argument is requested, the hearing will be at 9 a.m. before Judge Kahn. Plaintiffs and judgment creditors Dale Duncan and Marta Mendoza's motion for appointment of Kevin Whelan as receiver and for a restraining order is granted. Plaintiffs, as judgment creditors of a large judgment, have shown good cause for the appointment of a limited receiver for the collection and payment to them of proceeds of the sales of real property assets of defendants and judgment debtors Anne Kihagi, Zoriall LLC, and Christina Mwangi. Except for the language on line 3 of page ten of their proposed order, plaintiffs have also shown good cause for their requested restraining order, which is in aid of their efforts to enforce their judgment. Plaintiffs' proposed restraining order is sufficiently precise and, but for the prohibition against communications with him or his employees and persons retained by him, is a measured restraint that is necessary and proper to enable the receiver to carry out his duties. Mr. Whelan is qualified to serve as the receiver. Defendants have not shown that Mr. Whelan is biased, will act prejudicially against their interests, or otherwise not follow the directions of the court. However, defendants may raise, on an ex parte basis if urgency is necessary, any objections they have about any actual or contemplated action of Mr. Whelan in the event that Mr. Whelan does act or proposes to act in a manner that shows he is biased, prejudiced against their interests, or otherwise not in compliance with the directions of the court. The fact that Mr. Whelan has consulted with and assisted plaintiffs' counsel with this motion and related matters does not, without more, show that he is unfit to be a receiver for this case. As the intended beneficiaries of the appointment of the receiver, plaintiffs' choice of receiver is entitled to great weight and there has been no showing sufficient to overcome the presumption that Mr. Whelan will faithfully comply with his oath and all court orders. Mr. Whelan must file a receiver's bond of $10,000 no later than March 8, 2019. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order, separate from the proposed order which they previously submitted, which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. If the tentative ruling is not confirmed, in addition to an order repeating verbatim the substantive portions of this tentative ruling, the court will sign the proposed order submitted by the plaintiffs after striking out the entirety of the language on line 3 of page 10 and inserting the following language under "Other Orders": "Nothing in this order prohibits or places limits on the rights of judgment creditors, their employees and agents to contact and communicate with the Receiver, is employees and agents." =(302/HK)

rulings.Case Title: NELLIE BERTUCCI ET AL VS. ANGELIQUE TAVARES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Robert Bertuccis To Defendants First Set Of Production Of Documents For Monetary Sanctions Against Robert Bertucci And His Counsel Of Record Separate Statement In Support Of Motion Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion Declaration Of Daniel Berko In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 2, DEFENDANT ANGELIQUE TAVARES Motion To Compel Further Responses By Plaintiff Robert Bertuccis To Defendants First Set Of Production Of Documents For Monetary Sanctions Against Robert Bertucci And His Counsel Of Record Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Complete Tentative Ruling has been emailed to all parties affected by this motion by Judge Pro Tem David Helbraun. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY ET AL VS. JENI FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Grace Image Photography, Judy Mckie'S Motion To Compel Further Responses (Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 3, Plaintiffs Grace Image Photography, Judy Mckie'S Motion To Compel Further Responses (Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 2) The motion to compel production of documents is denied without prejudice to renewal following genuine reasonable good faith meet and confer. Sanctions are denied to either party. The Court urges, but does not order, counsel to conduct an in-person meet and confer of at least one hour's duration in which they go through each and every RPD and discuss each objection - including the issues of overbreadth, ambiguity, and undue burden raised, as well as asserted privileges -- so as to narrow the discovery dispute to the irreducible minimum, before calling upon the Court to resolve the matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to dmh@helbraunlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY ET AL VS. JENI FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY, JUDY MCKIE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses (part 1 of 2) Pro Tem Judge David Helbraun, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion Denied in Part and Granted in Part: The moving papers and Declaration in Support thereof fail to show a reasonable and good faith effort to meet and confer regarding any issues other than that of the disputed electronic accounts. It is not demonstrated in the moving papers that sufficient reasonable and good faith efforts were made to address each objection to each Request so as to narrow the discovery dispute to the irreducible minimum, before calling upon the Court to resolve the matter. The moving papers also failed to attach to the supporting Declaration several of the items asserted to be contained there, including the subject Amended Responses. However, the subject Amended Responses are presented by defendants' opposition papers, and they clearly are not Code-compliant, in that they fail to definitively affirm that a thorough and diligent search has been conducted; whether responsive documents in the Party's possession or control exist as to each RPD; whether any such responsive documents will be produced as to each RPD, and whether any are withheld under a claim of privilege; or whether the Party is unable to respond because the requested items do not exist in their possession or control or have been destroyed or lost. Therefore, the Responding Parties are ordered to provide further, verified, amended Code-compliant written responses that definitively affirm that a thorough and diligent search has been conducted; whether responsive documents in the Party's possession or control exist as to each RPD; whether any such responsive documents will be produced as to each RPD, and whether any are withheld under a claim of privilege; or whether the Party is unable to respond because the requested items do not exist in their possession or control or have been destroyed or lost. If electronic accounts exist which either responding party has access to and the Requesting /Moving Party does not have access to, then any such accounts are to be identified and any responsive and non-privileged documents from that source(s) must be included in the written response. Further amended, verified, written responses to be served within 45 days. (End of Tentative Ruling Part 1 of 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY ET AL VS. JENI FONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, March 1, 2019, Line 11, PLAINTIFFS GRACE IMAGE PHOTOGRAPHY AND JUDY MCKIE'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction. Plaintiffs move under CCP Sec. 526(a)(3) to enjoin defendants from "destroying, deleting or secreting relevant evidence." The motion is denied. CCP Sec. 526(a)(3) may be used to enjoin acts in litigation that violate "the rights of another party," and, though not a tight fit, the statute has been applied to discovery. (Id.; Dodge, Warren & Peters Ins. Services, Inc. v. Riley (105 Cal.App. 4th 114, 1418-19.) However, Sec. 526(a)(3) speaks in the present tense - "is doing," "is about to do" - while the last "destroying, deleting or secreting" plaintiffs allege was in April 2018, over 10 months ago. The delay in Dodge was not nearly so protracted. A panoply of discovery and evidentiary sanctions are available if plaintiffs show discovery abuse, but CCP Sec. 526(a)(3) does not apply here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)
